speaker: RICHARD
text: Now is the winter of our discontent

speaker: RICHARD
text: Made glorious summer by this son of York,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And all the clouds that loured upon our house

speaker: RICHARD
text: In the deep bosom of the ocean buried.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Now are our brows bound with victorious wreaths,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Our bruisèd arms hung up for monuments,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Our stern alarums changed to merry meetings,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Our dreadful marches to delightful measures.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Grim-visaged war hath smoothed his wrinkled front;

speaker: RICHARD
text: And now, instead of mounting barbèd steeds

speaker: RICHARD
text: To fright the souls of fearful adversaries,

speaker: RICHARD
text: He capers nimbly in a lady’s chamber

speaker: RICHARD
text: To the lascivious pleasing of a lute.

speaker: RICHARD
text: But I, that am not shaped for sportive tricks,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Nor made to court an amorous looking glass;

speaker: RICHARD
text: I, that am rudely stamped and want love’s majesty

speaker: RICHARD
text: To strut before a wanton ambling nymph;

speaker: RICHARD
text: I, that am curtailed of this fair proportion,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Cheated of feature by dissembling nature,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Deformed, unfinished, sent before my time

speaker: RICHARD
text: Into this breathing world scarce half made up,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And that so lamely and unfashionable

speaker: RICHARD
text: That dogs bark at me as I halt by them—

speaker: RICHARD
text: Why, I, in this weak piping time of peace,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Have no delight to pass away the time,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Unless to see my shadow in the sun

speaker: RICHARD
text: And descant on mine own deformity.

speaker: RICHARD
text: And therefore, since I cannot prove a lover

speaker: RICHARD
text: To entertain these fair well-spoken days,

speaker: RICHARD
text: I am determinèd to prove a villain

speaker: RICHARD
text: And hate the idle pleasures of these days.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Plots have I laid, inductions dangerous,

speaker: RICHARD
text: By drunken prophecies, libels, and dreams,

speaker: RICHARD
text: To set my brother Clarence and the King

speaker: RICHARD
text: In deadly hate, the one against the other;

speaker: RICHARD
text: And if King Edward be as true and just

speaker: RICHARD
text: As I am subtle, false, and treacherous,

speaker: RICHARD
text: This day should Clarence closely be mewed up

speaker: RICHARD
text: About a prophecy which says that “G”

speaker: RICHARD
text: Of Edward’s heirs the murderer shall be.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Dive, thoughts, down to my soul. Here Clarence comes.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Brother, good day. What means this armèd guard

speaker: RICHARD
text: That waits upon your Grace?

speaker: CLARENCE
text: His Majesty,

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Tend’ring my person’s safety, hath appointed

speaker: CLARENCE
text: This conduct to convey me to the Tower.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Upon what cause?

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Because my name is George.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Alack, my lord, that fault is none of yours.

speaker: RICHARD
text: He should, for that, commit your godfathers.

speaker: RICHARD
text: O, belike his Majesty hath some intent

speaker: RICHARD
text: That you should be new christened in the Tower.

speaker: RICHARD
text: But what’s the matter, Clarence? May I know?

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Yea, Richard, when I know, for I protest

speaker: CLARENCE
text: As yet I do not. But, as I can learn,

speaker: CLARENCE
text: He hearkens after prophecies and dreams,

speaker: CLARENCE
text: And from the crossrow plucks the letter G,

speaker: CLARENCE
text: And says a wizard told him that by “G”

speaker: CLARENCE
text: His issue disinherited should be.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: And for my name of George begins with G,

speaker: CLARENCE
text: It follows in his thought that I am he.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: These, as I learn, and such like toys as these

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Hath moved his Highness to commit me now.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Why, this it is when men are ruled by women.

speaker: RICHARD
text: ’Tis not the King that sends you to the Tower.

speaker: RICHARD
text: My Lady Grey his wife, Clarence, ’tis she

speaker: RICHARD
text: That tempers him to this extremity.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Was it not she and that good man of worship,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Anthony Woodeville, her brother there,

speaker: RICHARD
text: That made him send Lord Hastings to the Tower,

speaker: RICHARD
text: From whence this present day he is delivered?

speaker: RICHARD
text: We are not safe, Clarence; we are not safe.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: By heaven, I think there is no man secure

speaker: CLARENCE
text: But the Queen’s kindred and night-walking heralds

speaker: CLARENCE
text: That trudge betwixt the King and Mistress Shore.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Heard you not what an humble suppliant

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Lord Hastings was to her for his delivery?

speaker: RICHARD
text: Humbly complaining to her Deity

speaker: RICHARD
text: Got my Lord Chamberlain his liberty.

speaker: RICHARD
text: I’ll tell you what: I think it is our way,

speaker: RICHARD
text: If we will keep in favor with the King,

speaker: RICHARD
text: To be her men and wear her livery.

speaker: RICHARD
text: The jealous o’erworn widow and herself,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Since that our brother dubbed them gentlewomen,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Are mighty gossips in our monarchy.

speaker: BRAKENBURY
text: I beseech your Graces both to pardon me.

speaker: BRAKENBURY
text: His Majesty hath straitly given in charge

speaker: BRAKENBURY
text: That no man shall have private conference,

speaker: BRAKENBURY
text: Of what degree soever, with your brother.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Even so. An please your Worship, Brakenbury,

speaker: RICHARD
text: You may partake of anything we say.

speaker: RICHARD
text: We speak no treason, man. We say the King

speaker: RICHARD
text: Is wise and virtuous, and his noble queen

speaker: RICHARD
text: Well struck in years, fair, and not jealous.

speaker: RICHARD
text: We say that Shore’s wife hath a pretty foot,

speaker: RICHARD
text: A cherry lip, a bonny eye, a passing pleasing tongue,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And that the Queen’s kindred are made gentlefolks.

speaker: RICHARD
text: How say you, sir? Can you deny all this?

speaker: BRAKENBURY
text: With this, my lord, myself have naught to do.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Naught to do with Mistress Shore? I tell thee, fellow,

speaker: RICHARD
text: He that doth naught with her, excepting one,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Were best to do it secretly, alone.

speaker: BRAKENBURY
text: I do beseech your Grace to pardon me, and withal

speaker: BRAKENBURY
text: Forbear your conference with the noble duke.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: We know thy charge, Brakenbury, and will obey.

speaker: RICHARD
text: We are the Queen’s abjects and must obey.—

speaker: RICHARD
text: Brother, farewell. I will unto the King,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And whatsoe’er you will employ me in,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Were it to call King Edward’s widow “sister,”

speaker: RICHARD
text: I will perform it to enfranchise you.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Meantime, this deep disgrace in brotherhood

speaker: RICHARD
text: Touches me deeper than you can imagine.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: I know it pleaseth neither of us well.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Well, your imprisonment shall not be long.

speaker: RICHARD
text: I will deliver you or else lie for you.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Meantime, have patience.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: I must, perforce. Farewell.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Go tread the path that thou shalt ne’er return.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Simple, plain Clarence, I do love thee so

speaker: RICHARD
text: That I will shortly send thy soul to heaven,

speaker: RICHARD
text: If heaven will take the present at our hands.

speaker: RICHARD
text: But who comes here? The new-delivered Hastings?

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Good time of day unto my gracious lord.

speaker: RICHARD
text: As much unto my good Lord Chamberlain.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Well are you welcome to the open air.

speaker: RICHARD
text: How hath your Lordship brooked imprisonment?

speaker: HASTINGS
text: With patience, noble lord, as prisoners must.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: But I shall live, my lord, to give them thanks

speaker: HASTINGS
text: That were the cause of my imprisonment.

speaker: RICHARD
text: No doubt, no doubt; and so shall Clarence too,

speaker: RICHARD
text: For they that were your enemies are his

speaker: RICHARD
text: And have prevailed as much on him as you.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: More pity that the eagles should be mewed,

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Whiles kites and buzzards prey at liberty.

speaker: RICHARD
text: What news abroad?

speaker: HASTINGS
text: No news so bad abroad as this at home:

speaker: HASTINGS
text: The King is sickly, weak, and melancholy,

speaker: HASTINGS
text: And his physicians fear him mightily.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Now, by Saint John, that news is bad indeed.

speaker: RICHARD
text: O, he hath kept an evil diet long,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And overmuch consumed his royal person.

speaker: RICHARD
text: ’Tis very grievous to be thought upon.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Where is he, in his bed?

speaker: HASTINGS
text: He is.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Go you before, and I will follow you.

speaker: RICHARD
text: He cannot live, I hope, and must not die

speaker: RICHARD
text: Till George be packed with post-horse up to heaven.

speaker: RICHARD
text: I’ll in to urge his hatred more to Clarence

speaker: RICHARD
text: With lies well steeled with weighty arguments,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And, if I fail not in my deep intent,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Clarence hath not another day to live;

speaker: RICHARD
text: Which done, God take King Edward to His mercy,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And leave the world for me to bustle in.

speaker: RICHARD
text: For then I’ll marry Warwick’s youngest daughter.

speaker: RICHARD
text: What though I killed her husband and her father?

speaker: RICHARD
text: The readiest way to make the wench amends

speaker: RICHARD
text: Is to become her husband and her father;

speaker: RICHARD
text: The which will I, not all so much for love

speaker: RICHARD
text: As for another secret close intent

speaker: RICHARD
text: By marrying her which I must reach unto.

speaker: RICHARD
text: But yet I run before my horse to market.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Clarence still breathes; Edward still lives and reigns.

speaker: RICHARD
text: When they are gone, then must I count my gains.
